one_line: Diminutive honky-tonk star who dominated Grand Ole Opry for six decades.

best_quote.text: I'm little but I'm loud/I'm poor, but I'm proud/I'm countrified and I don't care who knows it.
best_quote.speaker: James Cecil Dickens

notable_dates.event: Birth of Little Jimmy Dickels in Bolt, West Virginia

headline: Dorrit Dekk obituary
name: Dorrit Dekk
one_line: Postwar graphic designer and "travel queen" known for Air France posters and public information campaigns.

best_quote.speaker: Dorrit Dekk
best_quote.text: Being an attractive woman usually working for male clients confused the issue, so she learned not to accept invitations to discuss commissions over dinner.

notable_dates.event: Hitler's occupation of Austria forced Dekk to flee Vienna in 1938
Birth date (noted as she celebrated design achievements throughout her career)

headline: Sonia d’Artois obituary
name: Sonia d'Artois
one_line: SOE courier and explosives expert who parachuted into occupied France before D-day.

best_quote.text: She knew they were forgeries and expected to be uncovered, but her confidence and fluency in colloquial French eventually persuaded them she was genuine.
best_quote.speaker: obit writer

notable_dates.event: Parachuted into France near Le Mans, nine days before D-day

headline: Ulrich Beck obituary
name: Ulrich Beck
one_line: German theorist of global risk and reflexive modernisation in unequal world.

best_quote.speaker: Ulrich Beck
best_quote.text: I was at a loss for an answer to the simple but necessary question: 'What is the meaning of the global events unfolding before our eyes?'

notable_dates.event: Born in Stolp, Germany (now Słupsk, Poland)

headline: Yolande Donlan obituary
name: Yolande Donlan
one_line: American actor who brought Hollywood glamour to British cinema with Val Guest.

best_quote.speaker: Val Guest
best_quote.text: Marry someone like Yolande.

notable_dates.event: Born Yesterday opened at the Garrick Theatre in London's West End

headline: Ron Henry obituary
name: Ron Henry
one_line: Left-back in Tottenham's 1960-61 League and FA Cup double-winning side

best_quote.speaker: Ron Henry
best_quote.text: I dream about football. I would have run all the way from here to Tottenham to play back then. It itches, you know.

notable_dates.event: Replaced Mel Hopkins as left-back after Hopkins' nose injury playing for Wales

headline: Edward W Brooke obituary
name: Edward William Brooke III
one_line: First African American elected to US Senate since Reconstruction era.

best_quote.text: I do not intend to be a national leader of the Negro people.
best_quote.speaker: Edward W Brooke

headline: Bernard Kay obituary
name: Bernard Frederic Bemrose Kay
one_line: Versatile character actor who brought gravitas to 100+ television roles over 60 years.

best_quote.text: I had Christianity beaten into me.
best_quote.speaker: Bernard Kay

headline: Frank Atkinson obituary
name: Frank Atkinson
one_line: Founder of Beamish, the pioneering open-air living museum of northern England.

best_quote.speaker: Frank Atkinson
best_quote.text: A museum's collection should not be limited by the size of its doors.

headline: Mario Cuomo obituary
name: Mario Matthew Cuomo
field: New York governor and Democratic politician
one_line: Populist Democrat who thrilled his party with a keynote speech, then twice shunned the presidency.

best_quote.speaker: Mario Cuomo
best_quote.text: God should not be made into a celestial party chairman.

notable_dates.event: Death of Mario Cuomo on the day his son Andrew was inaugurated for a second term as New York governor

headline: Lydia Mordkovitch obituary
name: Lydia Mordkovitch
one_line: Soviet-trained violinist who recorded over 60 works for Chandos label across repertoires.

best_quote.speaker: Lydia Mordkovitch
best_quote.text: Everything he did was balanced, in the right proportion. He never exaggerated, and he was so sincere. He worked mainly on interpretation, on repertoire.
